explanation: The requirement can be interpreted as: 'In PCV Mode (indicated by prop_0), if prop_1 holds, then prop_2 must also hold.' In LTL, this is typically written as 'Globally, whenever prop_0 and prop_1 are true, prop_2 must also be true.'

The statement indicates that at the moment prop_0 holds, if prop_1 holds, then prop_2 must also hold. This suggests a local implication at each point in time: if prop_0 and prop_1 are true, then prop_2 must be true at that point. This is globally (at all times) enforced.

The sentence states that at the time when 'prop_0' holds, if 'prop_1' also holds, then 'prop_2' must hold as well. This is effectively: Whenever both 'prop_0' and 'prop_1' are true at some position, 'prop_2' must also be true at the same position. In LTL, this can be written globally: G( (prop_0 & prop_1) -> prop_2 )

The statement expresses that at prop_0, if prop_1 holds, then prop_2 should hold as well. Interpreted in LTL, this means globally, whenever prop_0 and prop_1 are true together, prop_2 must also be true at the same point.

The sentence states that at the situation prop_0, if prop_1, then prop_2. Interpreting this in a temporal fashion, whenever prop_0 holds, it must be that if prop_1 also holds at the same time, then prop_2 must hold as well. This translates to the invariant: globally, if prop_0 holds, then (if prop_1 then prop_2).

The requirement states that 'At the end of an inspiration phase, if prop_0, prop_1', which indicates that whenever (at all times) we reach the end of an inspiration phase, if prop_0 and prop_1 hold, some (unmentioned) consequence happens. Since the 'consequence' is not specified, the translation focuses on the condition being true at the end of inspiration phases. Assuming we're just to encode the condition itself, we focus on the property: globally, at each relevant moment ('end of inspiration'), if we are at the end, then prop_0 & prop_1 holds. Since 'end of inspiration' is not an atomic proposition but is only elided here, the translation would code the implication directly with the information provided. If 'end of inspiration' corresponds to a specific instant, then 'prop_0 & prop_1' must hold at that instant. Thus, the translation is simply: G (prop_0 & prop_1)

The statement indicates that in PCV mode (represented by prop_0), if prop_1 holds, then prop_2 must also hold. This can be directly represented as: whenever prop_0 and prop_1 hold, then prop_2 must also hold. This is a safety property that should hold globally. We use G to indicate that it should hold at all times.

The specification refers to the end of an inspiration phase and a condition over prop_1 and prop_2. Assuming 'at the end of an inspiration phase' corresponds to an atomic proposition (not given), but as per given information, the LTL can be formulated as the following: Eventually (F), if both prop_1 and prop_2 hold, then some effect or condition follows. Since the full effect is not specified, a direct translation is 'eventually (prop_1 and prop_2)'.
The statement indicates that at 'prop_0', if 'prop_1' is true, then 'prop_2' should hold. This can be interpreted as: always, if 'prop_0' is true, then ('prop_1' implies 'prop_2').

The statement indicates that at prop_0, if prop_1, then prop_2. That is, whenever prop_0 is true, if prop_1 is true at the same moment, then prop_2 must also be true at that moment. This can be represented globally (G) as: whenever prop_0 holds, (prop_1 implies prop_2).
